text: Publicly available scientific resources provide the foundation for establishing evaluation standards, enhancing educational platforms, and ensuring experimental reproducibility. Version 4 of the Insight ToolKit (ITKv4) introduces a significant evolution in image registration methodology, aiming to set new open-source standards for the community. Compared to its predecessors, ITKv4 offers several key technical innovations, including support for multivariate images and objective functions. A primary advancement is the unification of high-dimensional (deformation field) and low-dimensional (affine) transformations. This is achieved through metrics that remain reusable across different transform types and the implementation of composite transforms, which allow users to chain arbitrary sequences of geometric mappings seamlessly. To improve efficiency, metrics and optimizers are now designed to fully exploit multi-core hardware. Furthermore, ITKv4 minimizes the burden of parameter tuning by introducing principled heuristics that automatically calculate scaling across disparate parameter types, such as rotations and translations, while also constraining step sizes for gradient-based optimization. Consequently, researchers can focus more on designing and comparing registration strategies rather than manual tuning. Ultimately, the ITKv4 framework serves as a robust structure for reproducible research and provides an extensive foundation for evaluating emerging registration techniques. We conclude by contextualizing these developments through a reference registration evaluation study applied to the labeling of pediatric brain images.
